title: Office-zombie alert: How to be job-ready after watching late-night FIFA World Cup 2026 matches? I asked ChatGPT
cleaned_news: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is gradually moving into the knockout zone, with matches scheduled for North American time zones leading to late-night and early-morning games that disrupt the work-life balance for Indian viewers, making it nearly impossible to stay sane in the office the next day, prompting a need for a practical, India-specific survival strategy for people who still have jobs, meetings and responsibilities to enjoy late-night FIFA World Cup matches without becoming useless at work.
bullets: • The FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 starts on 29 June.
• FIFA World Cup matches are scheduled for North American time zones, leading to late-night and early-morning games that disrupt the work-life balance for Indian viewers.
• To balance enjoying late-night FIFA matches with work, prioritize one premium match on office days and utilize weekends for extended viewership.
• Hydrating early, opting for lighter dinners, avoiding caffeine post-6 PM, and limiting screen time after matches can help mitigate sleep debt.
• A 3:30 AM kick-off before an office day calls for highlights, which is strategy, not failure.

vocabularies.meaning_en: Arousing pity, especially through vulnerability or sadness; miserably inadequate.
Make less severe, serious, or painful.
vocabularies.meaning_hi: कम करना, शांत करना, हल्का करना

vocabularies.meaning_en: Interrupt (an event, activity, or process) by causing a disturbance or problem.
vocabularies.meaning_hi: बाधित करना, भंग करना, अस्त-व्यस्त करना

vocabularies.meaning_en: Place or arrange (things) in a straight line; give support to (a person, organization, or cause).
vocabularies.meaning_hi: संरेखित करना, एक सीध में लाना, मेल खाना

error_spotting.sentence: Those late-night matches makes it nearly impossible to stay sane in the office the next day.
error_spotting.rule: Subject-verb agreement. The plural subject 'matches' requires a plural verb 'make', not the singular 'makes'.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: Hydrating early and opting for lighter dinners can also help _____ sleep debt.

jumbled_parts: A. The FIFA World Cup 2026 is gradually moving into the knockout zone.
B. The Round of 32 starts on 29 June.
C. Those late-night matches make it nearly impossible to stay sane in the office the next day.
D. Let’s admit, the timings are pathetic for viewers in India.

title: U.S. presses Meta to agree to AI reviews as security concerns rise: Report
cleaned_news: The Trump administration is pressing Meta to submit its AI models for voluntary review, which would allow the government to evaluate their abilities and vulnerabilities, as the Facebook parent is the only major U.S. developer of AI technology that has not reached an agreement to voluntarily share its models with the federal government for review, while concern is growing in Washington over the national security risks posed by powerful AI systems.
bullets: • The Trump administration is pressing Meta to submit its AI models for voluntary review.
• Meta is the only major U.S. developer of AI technology that has not reached an agreement to voluntarily share its models with the federal government for review.
• OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI have already agreed or are working with the U.S. government for AI model evaluations.
• Earlier this month, the U.S. government ordered Anthropic to suspend access to its most advanced AI models for foreign nationals, citing national security concerns.
• U.S. officials are aiming to identify threats ranging from cyberattacks to military misuse before AI tools are widely deployed.

vocabularies.meaning_en: weaknesses or susceptibilities to harm or attack.
the action of overseeing something; supervision.
the extreme limit of understanding or achievement in a particular area.

vocabularies.meaning_hi: तैनात किया गया, उपयोग किया गया

error_spotting.sentence: The Trump administration is pressing Meta to submit its AI models for voluntary review, which would allows the government to evaluate their abilities and vulnerabilities.
error_spotting.rule: After modal verbs like 'would', the base form of the verb (infinitive without 'to') must be used. 'Allows' is a third-person singular present tense form, which is incorrect here.

fill_in_the_blanks.sentence: The Trump administration is pressing Meta to submit its AI models for voluntary _____, which would allow the government to evaluate their abilities and vulnerabilities.

jumbled_parts: The Facebook parent, which launched the Muse Spark AI model in April,
that has not reached an agreement to voluntarily share its models
is the only major U.S. developer of AI technology
with the federal government for review, according to the report.
